findings.evidence: Grep hits: `BernsteinAux.lean:67-69` has `(hx : 0 ≤ x) (hxT : x < T)`, `BernsteinAux.lean:92-94` has `(hx : 0 ≤ x) (hxT : x < T)`, and `BernsteinAux.lean:118-119` has `(hT : 0 < T)`.
findings.fix: Weaken these hypotheses to `hxT : x ≤ T` and `hT : 0 ≤ T`, splitting off `x = T` or `T = 0`; propagate the same weakening to `integral_neg_deriv_Ici` in `BernsteinIntegral.lean`.
findings.issue: `neg_one_pow_mul_taylor_remainder_nonneg`, `integral_neg_deriv`, and `integral_mass` require `x < T`/`0 < T`; the natural interval-integral statements are valid for `x ≤ T`/`0 ≤ T`, with the equal-endpoint case reducing to a zero integral.

summary: The interval-integral API is slightly under-general: exported lemmas require strict endpoint inequalities even though the natural statements include the degenerate interval case.
